intro: Ctenopteris denticulata, also known as the denticulate maidenhair fern, is a species of fern found in Southeast Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique tooth-like serrations on its leaves. Traditionally, Ctenopteris denticulata has been used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenopteris denticulata is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or made into a tincture or capsule, and is commonly used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
